reasoning:
On September 29, 2025, EA announced it had reached a definitive agreement to be acquired by an investor consortium (led by Saudi Arabia's PIF and Silver Lake) in an all-cash $55 billion transaction valuing the shares at $210.00 each. The deal is expected to close in mid-2026. The current stock price (as of the last trading day before January 4, 2026) is approximately $204.22. Because this is an all-cash acquisition at a premium, the stock price will structurally drift upwards toward the $210 target as the time value of money decreases the arbitrage spread. Furthermore, standard dividend payments have been suspended, eliminating the risk of ex-dividend price drops. This upward drift makes it more likely than not (>50%) that the stock will be higher than $204.22 on future dates. 

However, PIF's involvement as a Saudi sovereign wealth fund buying a major US tech and cultural asset introduces significant regulatory risk, particularly from CFIUS (Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States). If CFIUS launches an in-depth investigation or blocks the deal, the stock price would plummet. While the short-term outlook (Jan 11) is relatively safe from major deal-breaking news and will benefit from the slow upward drift, the cumulative probability of adverse regulatory news triggering a spread-widening event (price drop) increases as we move further into the anticipated closing window. Thus, following the instruction that uncertainty should increase over time, probabilities for the more distant dates (Feb 3 and Apr 4) are modeled closer to 0.5 to reflect the escalating risk of deal delays or termination.
